intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer calming, desaturated palettes over vibrant ones to reduce cognitive load during regular check-ins
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use a restful, low-saturation color scheme (grays, dusty blues, warm beiges) to create a contemplative atmosphere for long-term health reflection
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either bold/saturated colors for energy, or a more clinical/neutral aesthetic—suggests they want the dashboard to feel either more stimulating or more utilitarian

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments may prefer snappy, data-first feedback over fluid micro-interactions
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — animated transitions vs instant static updates
facade.label: Instant numbers, no animation
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and clarity over delight; dashboard should prioritize reading metrics fast, skip entrance animations, use static value updates
facade.rejectImplies: User wants some motion or polish; dashboard could include subtle transitions, value counters, or smooth state changes to feel less clinical

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer calming, desaturated palettes over vibrant ones to reduce cognitive load during regular check-ins
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use a restful, low-saturation color scheme (grays, dusty blues, warm beiges) to create a contemplative atmosphere for long-term health reflection

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing one key metric prominently displayed over dense narrative explanations of their health data
facade.axisTargeted: Numbers vs narrative — should health dashboards lead with single powerful metrics or contextualized story-driven insights?
facade.label: Big number, small story
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should feature large, scannable primary metrics (weight, steps, sleep hours) with minimal supporting text underneath
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should embed numbers within narrative context — charts with captions, trends explained through story arcs, metrics justified by their meaning rather than their size

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent left navigation paired with card-based content layout for scanning experiments and metrics
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + content grouping (sidebar vs tabs; cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore persistent sidebar navigation in next probes; test card density and spacing; consider what lives in sidebar vs main area
facade.rejectImplies: Test horizontal tabs or center-focused layouts; explore list-based metric displays; reconsider persistent vs collapsible navigation

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable visual language over clinical or technical aesthetics
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs. geometric — rounded corner radius and visual warmth
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use generous border-radius, avoid sharp corners; signals preference for friendly, less clinical tone; likely accepts warm color palettes and cushioned spacing
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer crisp, minimal, or more structured aesthetic; possible lean toward flat design, sharp edges, or clinical precision; suggests dashboard should feel more professional/technical than inviting

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a conversational, low-pressure tone for logging health data—like texting a friend rather than filling out a form
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, conversational copy; informal language; emoji accents; approachable error states; personality in microcopy
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward clinical, formal tone; medical terminology; minimal decoration; corporate-neutral voice; data-first presentation

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments may find that subtle, human-made imperfections (like hand-sketched accent elements or organic typography) create emotional warmth and trust, making the dashboard feel like a personal journal rather than a clinical tool
facade.label: Handwritten details
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should incorporate subtle hand-drawn or script elements (handwritten date labels, sketched flourishes, organic line weights) to reinforce the intimate, personal nature of health tracking and reduce the clinical sterility of pure digital interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a clean, purely digital aesthetic without any hand-drawn or organic typography elements—suggesting they want clarity and precision over emotional warmth in their health tracking interface
